image_prompt: SVG: smooth exponential decay graph, number of americium-241 nuclei (y-axis 0 to 10 000, major every 1000, minor every 500) vs time / years (x-axis 0 to 2000, major every 200, minor every 50). Curve starts at (0, 10 000) and follows N(t) = 10000 x 0.5^(t/420), giving a half-life of 420 years: passes through (420, 5000), (840, 2500), (1260, 1250), (1680, 625). Teal smooth line, fine gridlines.

SVG: four trend-line graphs (A, B, C, D) of resistance / ohm (y-axis 0 to 140, major every 20, minor every 5) vs diameter / mm (x-axis 5 to 30, major every 5, minor every 1). All four panels plot the same five data points (8, 128), (12, 56), (16, 32), (20, 20), (24, 14) as teal crosses. A: straight dot-to-dot segments (jagged). B: a single straight best-fit line through the cloud. C: a smooth curve that under-fits and misses the points. D: a smooth inverse curve passing through every point (the correct trend line).
SVG: four labelled panels A-D for "which does NOT show periodic motion" MCQ.

render_data.title: Periodic motion — Earth orbiting the Sun
render_data.caption: Earth's orbit repeats every 365 days — regular, repeating movement, so it IS periodic motion (like a swinging pendulum). The MCQ asks which option does NOT show periodic motion: option C (a one-off, non-repeating motion) is the odd one out. Correct answer: C.

topic: Available rods tray (Materials A, B, C, various diameters)
image_prompt: SVG: isometric tray labelled "Available rods" with assorted rods of three materials at various diameters. Variant: rods instead of cylinders.

render_data.title: Select the graphite rods to collect appropriate data
render_data.prompt: To answer the research question, vary the diameter ONLY — keep material and length constant. Sort each available rod into the correct group: use five rods of one material (graphite, Material A) at five different diameters; leave the rest in the tray.

topic: Student notebook raw data (rod diameter and resistance, variant values)
image_prompt: SVG: notepad with handwritten data entries: 16 mm/32 Ω, 8 mm/128 Ω, 24 mm/14 Ω, 12 mm/56 Ω, 20 mm/20 Ω (rod diameters and resistance, variant values).
